intro: Rhyncospermum jasminoides variegatum, also known as Star Jasmine or Trachelospermum jasminoides variegatum, is a flowering evergreen vine native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and variegated leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhyncospermum jasminoides variegatum include tracheloside, trachelospermumine, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote relaxation.
